title: verbascum wilhelmsianum
intro: Verbascum wilhelmsianum, also known as Wilhelms' mullein, is a biennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Verbascum wilhelmsianum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical ointment, and is also used in tinctures and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
